title: Glassy-Jelly Dutch Lazy Sunday Dish Paardebloem
description: A deliciously jammy, glassy-jelly Dutch dish perfect for a lazy Sunday, featuring the flavors of cilantro and a crispy agemono-deep-fry in a low-fat-friendly tandoor-clay-pot.
instructions: Combine Paardebloem, water, sugar, and lemon juice in a large bowl, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Heat the vegetable oil in a tandoor-clay-pot over medium-high heat.
Deep-fry the Paardebloem mixture in batches until crispy and golden brown.
Drain the Paardebloem mixture on paper towels and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Mix the deep-fried Paardebloem mixture with the lemon juice and sugar mixture in a serving bowl.
Refrigerate the mixture for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves before serving.
This dish can be served as a breakfast or brunch option on a lazy Sunday morning.
This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner when you need something quick and easy to prepare.
This dish can be made ahead of time and refrigerated or frozen for later use.
This dish is a great way to use up leftover Paardebloem or other ingredients in your pantry.
This dish is a delicious and unique twist on a traditional Dutch dessert.
This dish is a great option for a potluck or buffet.
This dish is a fun and flavorful way to spend a lazy Sunday afternoon in the kitchen.
This dish is a great way to practice making agemono-deep-fry in a tandoor-clay-pot.
